rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR MIROV VS. ARKADY BERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT ALEX VARUM'S MOTION TO DISMISS. Denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 583.210 does not require that Alex Varum be dismissed. Because Mr. Varum has been sued as an alter ego of an existing defendant, section 583.210 is inapplicable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK KELLY VS. THOMAS WHITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Patrick Kelly'S Motion To Compel Responses And To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted By Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 36. Plaintiff Patrick Kelly's Motion To Compel Responses And To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted By Defendant. Continued to September 11, 2012 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer in an effort to either resolve or minimize their disputes. In undertaking their meet and confer efforts, the parties should discuss, among other thins, significantly narrowing the number of plaintiff's discovery requests and be guided by the following: 1) the number of discovery requests propounded by plaintiff is excessive, burdensome and unnecessary; 2) form interrogatory 15.1 likely provides a vehicle for plaintiff to learn most or all of the information he seeks by way of contention discovery; and 3) defendant's boilerplate objections constitute discovery abuse. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 10. PLAINTIFF SALMA KHAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Second Amended Complaint. Granted. The strong policy of permitting amendments to pleadings and the fact that any prejudice to defendant by the addition of the new claims can be eliminated by a trial continuance supports the granting of the motion. If defendant desires, the court will condition granting the motion on the continuance of the trial to a mutually agreeable date in the spring or summer of 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling is not contested and the parties agree on a new trial date, the new trial date should be included in the proposed order. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL, AS TRUSTEE OF THE JOHN VS. COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 18. DEFENDANTS COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534), CLYDE COURNALE, CURTIS COURNALE, WILLIAM SCHMETTERER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Denied as to Defendant Clyde Cournale and granted with fifteen days leave to amend as to the first, second, third, and fourth causes of action. There are sufficient allegations as to Defendant Clyde Cournale. Plaintiff is given leave to amend the first cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege that all of the holders of notes agreed in writing to be bound by the procedure outlined in section 2941.9 and that such agreement was recorded or that recording was not made and should not be required. Plaintiff is given leave to amend the second cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a basis for seeking attorney's fees and ultimate facts sufficient to support the claim. Plaintiff is given leave to amend the third and fourth causes of action to amend to plead whether the alleged contract is written, oral or implied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SALAZAR, ANA MARICELA A.K.A. MARICELA A. BUITRAGO VS. JAJEH, XIOMARA FATIMA AS ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 19. PLAINTIFF ANA MARICELA SALAZAR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Because this case involves allegations of unpaid child support, it should be brought in family court, not civil court. Moreover, even if this is a proper civil action, the motion should be denied due to a triable issue regarding how much, if any, child support is owed. Defendant's request for sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 22. PLAINTIFF GEETA BHADAURIA'S Motion For Order To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Shapiro as follows: Denied because responses were served after the motion was filed. Plaintiff is awarded monetary sanctions of $1,000 against Defendant Amar Alhanshali and his attorneys to be paid by October 1, 2012. rulings.Case Title: JEREMY RICKS VS. CARDREADY LLC, et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For An Order Directing The Default Clerk To Cancel Filing Of Mtn To Transfer Mtn
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 24. PLAINTIFF JEREMY RICKS'S Motion For An Order Directing The Default Clerk To Cancel Filing Of Motion To Transfer. Denied. The motion is, in practical effect, one for reconsideration yet it is not based on any new or different facts, law or circumstances and otherwise is not in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 1008. Moreover, even if the court were to reconsider its prior ruling, the court would re-affirm that ruling, not vacate it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SIMPSON MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. et al VS. NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 25. DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Stay Action. Granted, but on different grounds than urged by the moving party. Per Montrose Chem. Corp. v. Superior Court (1993) 6 Cal.4th 287, this action, including the cross-complaint of Fireman's Fund, is stayed pending the resolution of the underlying actions. Because the court rules on Montrose grounds, the court does not address the forum nonconveniens grounds raised by the moving party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MYERS VS. JENNY PEI LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Motion For Reconsideration Appointing Receiver And For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 29. PLAINTIFF FRANK MYERS'S Second Amended Motion For Reconsideration Appointing Receiver And For Preliminary Injunction. Denied. Although the notices of violation tend to show that a receiver may be necessary to preserve the property, Plaintiff essentially seeks appointment of a receiver so that he may recover money damages for breach of contract. That is not a proper basis for the extraordinary remedy of a receiver where, as here, the party seeking the appointment of a receiver has not shown that he has any right to or interest in the property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION VS. BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2012, Line 31. DEFENDANT BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC, BRANDY HO, JIMMY LAM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first and fourth causes of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the third cause of action. The first and fourth causes of action are adequately pled. The third cause of action fails to allege that Ho and Lam have an ownership interest in the corporation. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, sufficient facts to cure this defect. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the documents sought to be judicially noticed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
